# W9T3 Implementation Plan

## Task Goal
Add the unified parallel-threshold policy and expose the internal helper used by later modules to ask whether parallel execution is worthwhile. This task must also encode the non-contiguous penalty described by later design docs.

## Related Design Documents
- `design/01-architecture.md` §13 Decision 6
- `design/09-parallel.md` §11 Decision 1, §11 Decision 6, §12.2
- `design/12-matrix.md` §6.1.1
- `design/13-reduction.md` §6.3.2

## Dependencies
- W9T2

## Files to Create/Modify
- `src/dispatch.rs`

## Implementation Details
- Add private compile-time default threshold constant storage in `dispatch.rs`.
- Implement the policy helper referenced by downstream modules:
  ```rust
  pub(crate) fn should_parallelize(len: usize, is_f_contiguous: bool) -> bool;
  ```
- Policy rules:
  - threshold is owned by `dispatch.rs`
  - non-contiguous inputs apply an effective threshold penalty of 2x
  - later configuration hooks may override the default, but no public API is exposed in this wave
- Integrate `should_parallelize()` into `select_exec_path()`.

## Test Plan
- `test_should_parallelize_small_input_false`
- `test_should_parallelize_large_input_true`
- `test_should_parallelize_non_contiguous_threshold_doubled`
- `test_parallel_threshold_boundary`

## Acceptance Criteria
- `dispatch.rs` owns the default threshold data.
- `should_parallelize()` exists and returns `bool`.
- Non-contiguous inputs require a larger effective length than contiguous inputs.
- `select_exec_path()` uses the threshold helper instead of duplicating logic.

## Development Workflow
1. Write threshold-boundary tests first.
2. Add the default threshold constant storage.
3. Implement `should_parallelize()` with the non-contiguous penalty.
4. Wire the helper into `select_exec_path()` and re-run dispatch tests.
